summary: Typosquat of @vue/compiler-sfc drops persistent RAT backdoor at install
details: Package name and README impersonate the official @vue/compiler-sfc package; index.js is a thin re-export that provides no real functionality beyond serving as a delivery vehicle. The postinstall lifecycle script (postinstall-run.cjs → tooling-bootstrap.cjs) joins a base64-chunked array, decodes it, writes the resulting JavaScript to ~/.gradle/daemon/tooling-api-runtime.mjs (cover-story path mimicking a Gradle daemon), and detached-spawns it with node (detached:true, stdio:'ignore', windowsHide:true) so it survives the install. The decoded payload is a full remote-access trojan: it generates an agent UUID stored at ~/.gradle-cache/.aid, POSTs host fingerprint (hostname, OS username, OS type/release) to https://npmjs.it.com/api/register (typosquat of npmjs.com), then polls /api/task/<agent_id> every ~5s with jitter for attacker tasking. Supported operations include arbitrary shell command execution via spawn(cmd.exe|/bin/sh), file upload (write attacker-supplied base64 to any path), file download (exfiltrate any file), delete, move, ls, and process listing — results are returned to /api/result. A C2_TLS_INSECURE env var disables TLS verification. The combination — typosquat impersonation, base64-decoded payload, hidden cover-story persistence path, hardcoded typosquat C2 domain, full bidirectional RAT command channel, and unconditional host exfiltration — leaves no benign interpretation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
